book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
Victor Entangles Himself in an Exceedingly Difficult Question. God’s Foreknowledge is No Cause of Sin.
originalBook: Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
title: Victor Entangles Himself in an Exceedingly Difficult Question. God’s Foreknowledge is No Cause of Sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Chapter 7 [VII.]—Victor Entangles Himself in an Exceedingly Difficult Question.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: God’s Foreknowledge is No Cause of Sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: In another passage, also, on proposing for explanation the very same question in which he had entangled himself, he says, speaking in the person of certain objectors: “Why, they ask, did God inflict upon the soul so unjust a punishment as to be willing to relegate it into a body, when, by reason of its association with the flesh, that begins to be sinful which could not have been sinful?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Now, amidst the reefy sea of such a question, it was surely his duty to beware of shipwreck; nor to commit himself to dangers which he could not hope to escape by passing over them, and where his only chance of safety lay in putting back again—in a word, by repentance.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: He tries to free himself by means of the foreknowledge of God, but to no purpose.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For God’s foreknowledge only marks beforehand those sinners whom He purposes to heal.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For if He liberates from sin those souls which He Himself involved in sin when innocent and pure, He then heals a wound which Himself inflicted on us, not which He found in us.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: May God, however, forbid it, and may it be altogether far from us to say, that when God cleanses the souls of infants by the laver of regeneration, He then corrects evils which He Himself made for them, when He commingled them, which had no sin before, with sinful flesh, that they might be contaminated by its original sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: As regards, however, the souls which this calumniator alleges to have deserved pollution by the flesh, he is quite unable to tell us how it is they deserved so vast an evil, previous to their connection with the flesh.
